name: Fire Bush
description: While it may look delicious, eating Fire Bush leaves has dire effects, causing the consumer to produce eye-watering flatulence.

name: Travelers' Sign
description: Beware! This sign likes to play switcheroo with its arrows. Where you're going all depends on where you are.

name: Saggle Tree
description: Monsters must be careful around the Saggle Trees. Their tasty nuts are too delicious-looking to resist, but they taste like rocks. Yuck!

name: Beeyoot Tree
description: Monsters should know better than to look directly at a Beeyoot Tree. When observed, the tree goes full-on supermodel, posing and contorting. Weird.

name: Smunkin Patch
description: What could be more comforting than cuddling with a ripe, orange Smunkin? Just don't let any Thumpies near; they like to carve out faces on them. What a strange thing to do.

name: Hollow Log
description: It's a Hollow Log. Maybe something cool will move in.

name: Bloofi Tree
description: Don't look too closely at the Bloofi Tree. Its feathery leaves have been 'borrowed' for some unknown purpose, leaving embarrassing bald patches.

name: Razzli Tree
description: Razzli Trees attract lots of things; iridescent bugs, slithery slugs, and especially monsters. Up close, the tree is kind of boring, and all the creatures leave.

name: Fuzzle Tree
description: The Fuzzle Tree loves it when monsters have long picnic lunches under its branches. Sometimes it grabs a snack from the basket when no one is looking.

name: Piney Tree
description: Monsters decorate Piney Trees every year for the Festival of Yay. Much to the tree's relief, monsters do not decorate well, and the objects fall off immediately.

name: Puffle Tree
description: This tree has the highest accident rating of all the trees. Its feather-like foliage tickles anyone who attempts to climb it until they fall out laughing.

name: Castanevine
description: When a strong breeze blows, the Castanevine really comes to life. Monsters sometimes blow at the plant just to hear it play.

name: Yum Yum Tree
description: Yum Yum Trees jiggle delightfully when gently shaken. Caution is advised, however, as the globes break easily, drenching the shaker with a sap as sticky as bubblegum.

name: Crumpler Tree
description: Old timers say never to go near a Crumpler Tree at night or you will face the wrath of the Crumplings, invisible fairies that leave you covered with itchy bites. What do old timers know, anyhow?

name: Zuffle Tree
description: Zuffle Trees bear delicious fruit that closely resembles the material pulled from a hairbrush. Those who consume it produce a melodic gacking sound.

name: Floofy Nest
description: Mischievous creatures, Floofies are sometimes mistaken for sock fluff. Have you ever seen sock fluff jump around like that?

name: Inverdigus Fern
description: The spooky yet breathtaking Inverdigus plant is truly a sight to behold. Mostly because you can bet it is using its baleful eyestalks to behold you at the same time.

name: Mountain Morsel
description: This relic isn't from around these parts. After a few experimental licks, it was discovered it tastes like grape bubblegum. The visages carved into its surface occasionally emit a low hum when monsters aren't looking.

name: Tree Hut
description: Some monsters like to haul their stuff around with them and need a place to put it for the day.

name: Barbloo Statue
description: This statue immortalizes the bravery with which its model saw something scary, pointed, and knelt in fear.

name: Thumpies Totem
description: Carved out of lumber harvested from the Great Tree of the Thumpieverse, this totem pays tribute to three famous Thumpies: KarrToof, RhooTz, and ChoMPh.

name: Babayag Tower
description: This tower would be more fun if it would just stand still and allow itself to be climbed! Still, it looks pretty.

name: Squeed Statue
description: Everyone loves a Squeed!

name: Dragoon Statue
description: The Dragoon was a harmless creature with a poor understanding of effective camouflage. Its resemblance to a bodiless claw made it such a prized collector's item that it rapidly became extinct.

name: Tree Forte Tower
description: The umbrella roof makes this a great tower for rainy days.

name: Bass String Bridge
description: Who can resist the charm of an old bridge over a tinkling freshet, all in the shade of a drooping snuffle tree?

name: Cozee Cabin
description: It's nice to have a place to take a quick nap. The Cozee Cabin isn't nearly as comfy as the Castle, but it'll do in a pinch.

name: Miniature Island Complex
description: Originally constructed as a tribute to Colossals and Titans alike, this work of art now houses many small creatures. Combining the Fire and Magical Islands into a Miniature Island Complex has created a Utopia for these tiny beings to flourish. Whether it's looking through the windowed eyes, or taking a relaxing dip in the Sanctum Swimming Pool atop the structures, there is something for all these little critters to do - including some kind of invite-only shindig that Crumplings are always crashing.

name: Leafy Sea Dragon
description: Monsters generally agree that this is a ridiculous fantasy creature. Nonetheless, they still make wishes and toss their teeth in the fountain.

name: Tub Fountain
description: The grimacing figure in this sculpture is based on a figure from monster bedtime stories, who would bathe and clean misbehaving monsterlings until there was no more dirt and grime on them. What a terrible fate.

name: Stritch Skin
description: What unfortunate creature gave up its hide for this device? None! This very-much-alive domesticated critter suspended itself from a frame and hangs there. No one knows why.

name: Flappy Flag
description: Who doesn't love to let their flag fly? Even better if it flaps and snaps in the wind!

name: Toob
description: Not for the faint of heart, a Toob oozes fragrant black goo. Monsters delight in daring one another to give the wheel a spin!

name: Pipes of Cicado
description: The Pipes of Cicado have amazing summoning power. When they are blown, everyone comes from all around, no matter what they may have been doing.

name: Eerie Remains
description: Monsters are known to plant flowers in and around Eerie Remains to show respect for the deceased.

name: Kab's Wax
description: The identity of the mysterious figure Kab has been hotly debated for eons - almost as hot as the bubbling wax held inside its famous buckets! The leading theory is that Kab is a time- and space-faring artisan that once proffered counsel to the Colossal of Amber Island. While in search of special capsules for Fire Monsters' essence, Kab's wax-sealed vessel design was chosen as the most viable. The entity's influence is felt even in the modern era, as the Fire Monsters of Fire Haven feel instinctually compelled to create art in the form of buckets and fill them with the wax of the island's many candles.

name: Digger
description: This Digger looks like it's emerging from the earth. How exciting!

name: Fossil-osaurus
description: Curious monsters often sing to the Fossil-osaurus, hoping to wake it up. Little do they know it's just a fiberglass model from some long-ago time.

name: Odd-o-Mobile
description: Despite its eerie appearance, the Odd-o-Mobile outshines most other nearby objects by dangling beautiful baubles from its finger-like appendages. It spends most of its time burrowing underground, only popping up whenever Monsters are struggling to fall asleep and need to catch some Z's. Once surfaced, its colorful bric-o-brac helps lull them into a comfortable sleep. The Odd-o-Mobile prefers its subterranean dwellings since the sun would damage its light-sensitive skin - though it enjoys the company of Monsters above ground enough that it appears a little faded.

name: Bottomless Pit
description: Monsters love to throw small objects into the bottomless pit. Sometimes an interesting sound or smell comes back up!

name: Optiglowbe
description: The existence of the Optiglowbe indicates that there are more varieties of Glowbe living in their home dimension than previously reckoned! This specimen appears to be more advanced than the others - some have speculated that it is related in some way to the critters of Psychic Island, and may even share their ability to surveil and record its surroundings.

name: Wubbox Stockpile
description: Because the Wubbox is part machine, it sometimes needs to replace the pieces of its body that have been worn down by the electric energy coursing through it. Other monsters have taken it upon themselves to set up tarpaulin lean-to's to protect the Wubbox's spare parts. Mysteriously, the markings painted on the tarp appear all by themselves.

name: Jarhead
description: It can take a while to get used to the Jarhead. But the monsters keep it around because despite its fearsome appearance, it has a gentle heart. Even though they're pretty sure that it only has a brain.

name: Englobulated F.U.M.
description: This creature, or "Fortuitously Unidentified Monster", has been right under our noses for... who knows how long! It is rumored that Dr. Wardin Spurrit spent an extensive amount of time trying to learn more about this apparently one-of-a-kind specimen, claiming to have witnessed its silhouette multiple times while studying the Rift, but it was near impossible to track due to its tendency of being able to "hop" between the endless alternate dimensions of which encircle the Monster World. However, the effort was revitalized quite recently, and after a fortuitous encounter - the details of which we've been told shall remain murky, miry even - we’ve been able to study it and learn about its cryptic nature. It is assuredly highly intelligent, deeming itself "a Monster, of course" but one that is “too sophisticated to sing”, despite our campaigns of encouragement. It has stated that it prefers to be kept in dormancy inside this mechanism, so it can "ponder and think to itself". According to data collected vis-à-vis brainwaves, exactly what it mulls over 24/7 is "beyond your comprehension and none of your Humbugswax".

name: Meldablend
description: Two very different objects seem to have materialized in the same place!

name: Trumplite
description: Many monsters find the dark pretty scary. They'll never need to turn this light on, but it's comforting to have it around.

name: Harpsitree
description: The Harpsitree plays hauntingly beautiful melodies to anyone nearby. You'll often see a Monster quivering its lower lip or gushing fat tears as it listens to a particularly moving passage.

name: Guitree
description: When an unwary monster trips over the strings of a Guitree, the most delightful sights and sounds are produced.

name: Reflecting Pool
description: Legend has it that an ancient monster created this reflecting pool to see if it had any spots on its back. Time has worn away the stone details and vines have grown around it. We still don't know about the spots.

name: Wild Bagpipe
description: Someone has captured the elusive wild bagpipe! It's been partially buried to prevent escape.

name: Phoni-Mine
description: Look! You can place an additional Mini-Mine on your islands now!... Wait, this is just a recreation of the cardboard constructable standee from My Singing Monsters: The Board Game? And there's a Furcorn popping out, recalling part of the scene from the accompanying art print? Well... alright then.

name: Cave Crystal Teleporter
description: This Teleporter's unstable portal appears to connect this world to the alternate dimension of Cave Crystal Island, where an enigmatic creature called the Connatruna dwells. The island occasionally flickers in and out of view - perhaps at the whims of the magical gem that floats above it - so take care as you're stepping through that you don't actually teleport yourself somewhere else!

name: Directions to Nowhere
description: Monsters love to interpret the mysterious meaning of this symbol. None of them can agree on a single way to read it, however, and the purpose is entirely unknown.

name: Spurrit Statue
description: A real Spurrit has not been spotted for a very long time. In fact, no one has ever seen one... or have they?

name: Harmonorb Holder
description: The Harmonorb is an odd object. Randomly found one day, the Monsters don't know what it is, but Monsters near it have felt more musically inspired than ever! So a replica has been made of it as well as a replica of the holder the Monsters made to, well, hold it. This replica has all the engraved, pretty patterns, and bits 'n' bobs the original holder had! The original orb and holder has influenced monster culture for generations. Just one thing -  where did this strange sphere come from? (It couldn't have come from the hooman world though, that's just a silly conspiracy theory!)

name: Orb of Aklino
description: What is this? Is it one of Wublin Island's numerous batteries? Is it an updated model for Galvana's staff? A Dyson Sphere? A Van de Graaff Generator? Or is it simply a cluttered agglomeration of different parts from a low budget sci-fi movie? Probably the latter, but it still looks cool!

name: Macho Monument
description: Gaze upon the majesty of the Macho Monument! This statue (named Steve) depicts a strongmon (NOT named Steve) and showcases what diligent training can accomplish. Some allege that the monument is actually an early draft of the Wubbox that never progressed any further. Either way - allow this fitness tribute to spark inspiration in your heart, so that, one day, you can be a Macho Mon too!

name: Shrini
description: The Mini-Shrine, or 'Shrini' for short, is an august creation of unknown origin which has the power to grant Monsters a vacation to the destination of their choosing. The opportunity doesn't come without some effort, though - a Monster must first collect tufts of the Sweetstreamz Tree, along with a special item that hints at where it wants to vacation. Then, it must deposit them in the bowl of the Shrini, strike the gong with the mallet, and POOF!… the Monster will dematerialize and travel to their vacation spot.

name: Spirit of Creation
description: When Colossals are engaged in an act of creativity, they often describe the experience among themselves as being guided by a 'spirit of creation.'  Depicted in ancient artworks as a kindly old being attuned to the power of nature, the spirit would whisper ideas and inspirations to its creator companion, and otherwise provide a backing track of gentle humming to which one could work. It was actually this piece of imagery that inspired the Colossals to imagine themselves as the guardians of the various islands of the Monster World, long before the cataclysm that ended the Dawn of Fire brought it about as reality.

name: Dragon Tower
description: Made from old dragon parts the monsters had laying around, this tower isn't very practical, but it looks great!

name: Ambered Thing
description: Hailing from the shard mountains of another dimension of the multiverse, this interesting specimen has been encased in amber. It answers to "Flutter," but the monsters usually just call it a thing.

name: 'Cannon Chaos' Contraption
description: This Decoration is a scale model of one of the cannons and cannonballs from the 'Cannon Chaos' game, which can be found inside the virtual world of the Playground inside of a Wubbox. Because it's powered by Electricity, maybe the Playground can connect digitally to other dimensions of the multiverse; we wonder if this activity was inspired by a visit to Toppelopolis?

name: 'Blast Off' Launchpad
description: This Decoration is a scale model of one of the rockets and launchpads from the 'Blast Off' game, which can be found inside the virtual world of the Playground inside of a Wubbox. Get ready for liftoff... oh dear, not enough energy. Maybe some other time. Up, Up and Away!

name: 'Monster Rally' Racetrack
description: This Decoration is a scale model of one of the racetracks from the 'Monster Rally' game, which can be found inside the virtual world of the Playground inside of a Wubbox. On your marks... get set... GO! Wow, a photo finish! Pure Skill!

name: Wub Hub Edifice
description: The Hub world of the Playground (or as it's coloquially referred to, the 'Wub Hub') is full of whimsical edifices like this one. The architectural styles of these buildings are inspired by designs from across the Monster World, but motifs and patterns from the archaic Starhenge seem to abound - unsurprising when you consider who designed the Wubbox...

name: Fandemonium Stage Maquette
description: This scale model of the special stage commemorates 8 amazing episodes of My Singing Monsters: Fandemonium! It's a balanced combination of Monster ingenuity and 'hooman' technology, perfect for broadcasting from the Monster World right into the comfort of your home!

name: Great Tree Bawnzi
description: This Bawnzi is a scaled-down version of the Great Tree that resides in the Thumpieverse. Several artistic interpretations of this arboreal landmark have existed throughout the years, with this latest iteration understood to be the most accurate representation. The sigils of the various Thumpie songs festoon the length of its meandering trunk, and there's even a replica of the little wooden door through which Thumpies access its hollow interior. It's thanks to the Magic of the Trees that binds the various dimensions of the Stablo Mjehurica, the World Tree, that contact between the Monster World and the Thumpieverse is even possible in the first place.

name: Spooktacle Smunkin Patch
description: Looks like no one heeded our advice and let Thumpies free in the Smunkin Patch. Just as we feared, they carved out faces on them. Strange.

name: Spooktacle Travelers' Sign
description: Beware! This sign has gotten into the spirit of the season - you never know quite whether you're going to get a trick or a treat.

name: Creepy Cauldron
description: This Spooktacular item is perfect for brewing potent potions and mysterious mixtures. We thought that those were bundles of wheat behind it - but maybe they're the shed silks of the Rare Oaktopus? It is a mad scientist, after all.

name: Frightful Fripperies
description: A chilling collection of eerie artifacts from the Spooktacle season. That skull bears a striking resembance to the Mythical Jarhead, a fearsome-looking entity that Monsters still aren't sure whether or not they dreamed up.

name: Smunk-o-Lantern Patch
description: Around the Spooktacle season, Monsters love to hollow out Smunkins and carve expressive faces into them, transforming them into Smunk-o-Lanterns. It is imperative that Monsters double check they aren't trying to pull up a sleeping Punkleton from the ground.

name: Inverdigus Cemetery
description: The spooky yet breathtaking Inverdigus plant is truly a sight to behold. Mostly because you can bet it is using its baleful eyestalks to behold you at the same time. This full-grown specimen has been carefully perched atop some decorative headstones and adorned with a fake shrunken head. At least, we hope it's fake.

name: Lamppost
description: The area around this lamppost was reportedly the ancient stomping grounds of a curious entity called Sir Sunmut. It was always babbling on about spare rooms and wardrobes, for some reason.

name: Coldman Colloquy
description: This trio of Coldmen (not snowmen, mind you) are content to converse amongst themselves. The cold never bothered them anyways.

name: Yaytide Trimmings
description: There's so much seasonal imagery in one Decoration! Bows, poinsettias, holly, ornaments - placing one of these on your island is a sure-fire way to keep the Yay spirit going strong.

name: Gingerbread Bluff
description: Modeled after the scrumptious seasonal coverings that adorn the Cold Colossal's icy horns, this edible edifice boasts stockings filled to the brim with surprises.

name: Luff-Balloons
description: Ninety-nine dreams we have had; in every one, either a purple, pink, or red balloon. These inflatable baubles emanate the power of Luff.

name: Curious Cultivar
description: The dilapidated fence surrounding this exotic plant isn't for its protection, it's for yours. More serpentine than rosaceous, it's not just thorns to be wary of on this specimen.

name: Gargle-oyle
description: This unusual object collects motes of dust floating through the sky, and transmutes them through arcane alchemical processes into candy hearts! Is it alive? We're not sure.

name: Chokkolit Fountain
description: Yum! Hewn from the Love Rocks of Air Island, this contraption has an elaborate mechanism for pumping liquid chokkolit to the top, which then flows down the sides for sweet-toothed Monsters to sample.

name: Rotten Egg
description: In the Monster World, being called a rotten egg is considered a compliment! After all, it means that you've been around a while and no one has thrown you out yet!

name: Floofy Basket
description: Because they already sort of look like eggs, the Floofies thought they'd conspire with the Blabbit to play a clever joke and get dressed up like Singing Monsters! How crafty of them.

name: Multiversal Phabberjay
description: Some painted eggs are more special than others. This 'Phabberjay', as it's called, features depictions of beings from across the multiverse.

name: Ginormous Carrot
description: Pareidolia is the phenomenon that describes seeing faces in inanimate objects, like this clearly-not-alive carrot of ginormous size. See, it even has a bite out of it. There's no way it cou-...

name: Granular Castle
description: While Singing Monsters prefer castles large enough to accommodate their beds, Granular Castles play host to all manner of miniature sand-dwelling critters. Looks like they're having a party in there!

name: Beach Chair
description: Ahh, nothing like stretching out on a relaxing beach chair. Remember to apply that sun screen - and try not to damage those surfboards, they feature vintage designs.

name: Hot Tub
description: Relax in the bubbling waters of this all-natural Hot Tub. A surprising number of Monsters can fit in it at once, so we suspect there's some magic at work here.

name: Hut of a Thousand Perfumes
description: A Monster's nose knows not which way to wend when sniffing the Hut of a Thousand Perfumes. Exotic fruits, fragrant flowers, ambrosial oils - all in all, an odoriferous experience for olfaction.

name: 1st Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the first year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the rediscovery of Ethereal Island at the year's end.

name: 2nd Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the second year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the announcement of Rare Monsters at the year's end.

name: 3rd Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the third year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the significance of Kayna's liberation from amber at the year's end.

name: 4th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the fourth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the incursion of the Mirror Islands' alternate timeline at the year's end.

name: 5th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the fifth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the homecoming of the Supernatural Wubbox on Wublin Island at the year's end.

name: 6th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the sixth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the unveiling of Epic Monsters at the year's end.

name: 7th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the seventh year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the mind-blowing reveal of Psychic Island at the year's end.

name: 8th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the eighth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the adventurous resettlement of the Colossingum at the year's end.

name: 9th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the ninth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the revelation of Seasonal Shanty at the year's end.

name: 10th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the tenth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the influx of special Mythical energies at the year's end.

name: 11th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the eleventh year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the Epic Wubbox's manifestation on Gold Island at the year's end.

name: 12th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the twelfth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the emergence of the Ethereal Quint from the Synthesizer on Ethereal Workshop at the year's end.

name: 13th Anniversary Monument
description: This commemorative decoration celebrates the thirteenth year of the Monster World's renewed contact with the human world. It displays pictograms of important events and milestones from that era, hosts a stone rendering of Jam Boree's candle, and highlights the new and improved Friends Menu and Profile feature that allow players to express themselves better at the year's end.

name: Monster Scareglowls
description: Despite their name, Scareglowls actually attract Glowls, not drive them away! The Fire Monsters will spot them from on high and swoop down to try and make conversation, only to discover that the prospective conversationalists are stuffed with hay.

name: Oversized Pies
description: Since it keeps frequent company with Fire Monsters, Gobbleygourd knows all sorts of exciting recipes from the era of the Dawn of Fire. Apple Tart, Pumpkin (ancestor of the contemporary Smunkin) Pie... although maybe someone went a little overboard on the portion sizes.

name: Carnivorous Cornucopia
description: What is it with Seasonal Decorations and pareidolia, seeing faces in inanimate objects? This particular object is maybe a little more animate than one would hope, so stick your hand in for a snack at your own risk.

name: Gobbley Inflatable
description: This balloon is from Fire Haven's welcome back party for Gobbleygourd, during the Seasonal Monster's first return to the island since its rediscovery. It has been specially inflated for Feast-Ember, but take care that there are no leaks anywhere... that would be embarrassing.

name: Makeshift Altar
description: This candelit [sic] altar holds drawings and keepsakes of lost loved ones. Full-scale ofrendas typically consist of 3 tiers, but the Monsters are hoping that the aroma of the cemasúchil adorning this makeshift display will be enough to draw the spirit back to the land of the livings.

name: Little Drummer
description: A small figure soundlessly mock-plays a special drum... but where's the beat? This is a reference to the heartbeat of forever, which can only be heard if your heart is open and you are listening very carefully.

name: Lady Calaca
description: Lady Calaca, Monsters at your feet... wonder how you manage to make ends meet! This towering statue is made of fired clay and enrobed in festive attire to celebrate the Beat Hereafter. Bone-Element Monsters have special techniques known only to them to achieve craftsMonship at this scale.

name: Heartstrings Stirring
description: A beautiful guitar too large for any Monster (except one that's been Biggified) rests gently on its stand. As the subterranean breeze blows, it passes through the papel picado and over the guitar's strings, stirring up nostalgic feelings of kinship and warmth.

name: Plentiful Planter
description: This is a miniature version of the Germination Station from Greatfruit Grove, where trees of all types were cared for and cultivated. Based on the purple and white leaves, there's currently a Polycule sapling in the planter - or it could be a Bazoom tree; honestly, it's hard to tell at this stage.

name: Golden Simian
description: In its native dimension, Greatfruit Grove was tended to by a troop of 'adorable little genius monkeys' who lived in the main tree, which they called the Monkey Mansion. These monkeys were responsible for the splicing that gave rise to so many fruit varieties, and the Golden Simian depicted on this fountain decoration was one such monkey.

name: Quaint Outhouse
description: All that fertilizer for the plant life of Echoes of Eco has to come from somewhere, and the Bovine Pegacorn is more than happy to oblige. Curiously, the Outhouse resembles Amber Island's Crucible - there's even a shape carved in the door of each - although their purposes vary drastically, as do the smells emanating from them.

name: Greatfruit Tree
description: The eponymous tree of Greatfruit Grove looks a little different depending on what dimension it's grown in, but an effort has been made to capture the spirit of the original with a carving bearing a jubilant face. The flavor of the fruit that this tree bears rivals that of any in the Monster World.

name: Floating Lanterns
description: This column of floating lanterns is decorated with imagery of fantastical creatures from other realms and clouds in the traditional xiangyun style. It's believed the lotus base emanates not only honesty, purity and beauty, but also the faint levitational powers required to keep the lanterns afloat.

name: Peony Wheel
description: Peonies are considered to be the king of flowers around the lunar new year, radiating wealth and good fortune. This circular 'wheel' arrangement, surrounded by vases and lucky bamboo, evokes a round window through which one might catch a glimpse of the upcoming year's success and prosperity.

name: Jade Lion
description: Just like the Leafy Sea Dragon, the 'Lion' is widely regarded as a fantasy creature, existing only in legend. The difference is that while the former is merely a speculative work of art, this jade sculpture is imbued with heavenly spirituality and resonates with a powerful blessing for all those nearby.

name: Magic Envelope
description: It's a tradition during Crescendo Moon to deliver red envelopes containing good luck wishes and "money warding off old age" (instrumental in Carillong's perpetual youth). The grandiose cutout display symbolizes a koi's journey upstream to become an auspicious dragon, the paragon of power and strength.

name: Fireworks Stand
description: Be sure to stand clear of the Fireworks Stand when it's showtime! While its flashy colours and sparkling lights may draw you in, all it takes is one lit fuse for the whole kit and caboodle to burst into an unforgettable performance of booms, bangs, and beauty. It is a beloved SkyPainting tradition to sneak surprise messages into your firework designs at special celebrations.

name: Kaleidoscope
description: The Kaleidoscope is not only a mesmerizing art installation, but also a reminder that every Monster looks at the world through its own lens. When all of these wondrously different perspectives combine, they create a magnificent mosaic that is much more than the sum of its parts. Many a Monster has lost track of time gazing into the Kaleidoscope's peaceful patterns.

name: Glowbe Guise
description: Did you know that Glowbes are bright in more ways than one? Within a Glowbe's big ol' globe lies a treasure trove of fun facts. This radiant Decoration honors Glowbe-kind's perpetual pursuit of the most titillating trivia they can uncover,

name: Prism Gate Replica
description: It's not a toy, it's a replica! This mint condition collector's item represents an impeccable imitation of the famed Prism Gate from Monster antiquity. This coveted piece even features a complete collection of all 6 prisment accesories. The Prism Gate's transformative qualities continue to spark the curiosity of Monsterkind to this very day.

name: Versegazing Telescope
description: Invented by the genius Rare Reebro, the Versegazing Telescope sees not just across distances, but dimensions! While its potential for making Island-shattering discoveries is second to none, its most common use is leisurely Tweedle-watching.

name: Blueprint Workshop
description: From sketch to sculpture, the Blueprint Workshop was once the birthplace of your favorite Monster species. The Colossals used all manner of tools, Bubblerite builds and Krystillium konstructs to craft Kazilleons and forge Fwogs, resulting in artisanship that was the envy of all during the Dawn of the [sic] Fire. Some craftMons even modeled their own Workshops after the original!

name: Teleportal
description: The Teleportal connects the fabled pocket dimension to the outside world. Its arrival is heralded by the appearance of a strange runic symbol seen throughout Monster antiquity. While countless semioticians have tirelessly researched its meaning, its true significance may be lost to time.

name: Graven Ghazt
description: Monsterkind has long pontificated about the creator - or creators? - behind these mysterious Graven Ghazts. A perplexing prophecy passed down through generations suggests that once the saucer of each statue is fitted with its matching orb, a passageway to another dimension will reveal itself.

name: Clover Contraption
description: Rather than being some kind of mechanical marvel, this 'contraption' is actually powered by magic alone; a potent spell has been cost upon it. A Cloverspell, if you will.

name: Pot of Enchanted Coins
description: At the rainbow's end, you'll occasionally find a pot like this one. Make no mistake - these are not the same Coins that are in circulation elsewhere in the Monster World. These are enchanted pieces that tantalize passersby but which dematerialize when they are removed from the pot.

name: Green Cupcakes
description: Using dye collected from verdant sources across the Monster World, these cupcakes of unusual size are the perfect color and flavor to celebrate the vernal equinox! Some have theorized that the Magical Rooba's diet consists entirely of these - but it turns out that's just moss.

name: Gigantic Metal Shoe
description: What size of creature could this shoe possibly accommodate? A juvenile Titan, perhaps? Touching the cool, metallic surface of the shoe confers a blessing of good luck - but only for a limited time.

name: Brain Teaser Tome
description: No Monster knows for certain what will transpire once the final puzzle in the Brain Teaser Tome is solved. Will the puzzler be showered in a rain of Diamonds, or stuffed with all the Cakes they can eat? Shockingly, the deep, dark truth is even more mindboggling: there is, in fact, no end to its puzzles. When one teaser is completed, a new page quietly appears.

name: Floating Library
description: Why settle for a boring ol' stuck-on-the-ground library when you can have a floating one? The Floating Library is the perfect hangout for airborne (or spaceborne) Monsters looking for a relaxing read. Just don't expect to find your books in the most logical sections; with tomes flying every which way, it's not the easiest library to organize.

name: Confounding Cryptex
description: To this day, no Monster has found the true meaning behind the complex symbols of the Confounding Cryptex - though countless have tried. It is widely believed that the solution lies within the fabled 'Dip Vinci Code', a sequence of Dipster sounds that will spring forth a special Key to unlock the Cryptex's hidden treasures... at least, in theory. The Dipsters prefer to mind their own Keys, thank you very much.

name: Bib'leo-Theque
description: Ooo Ay La Bib'leo-Theque? Right here! Part bookcase, part Bib'leo hangout, the Bib'leo-Theque is where restless Bib'leos go to make friends, have a nap or just snack on some chewy old bookmarks. You wouldn't know by looking at them, but Bib'leos are actually pack-oriented creatures who find comfort in company.

name: Helmet Rack
description: Heavy is the head that wears the helmet, and it's nice to feel the breeze on the top of one's head. The Spurrits prefer to be consistent in who wears which headgear, but that's not to say that they don't sometimes switch off - sometimes a change is as good as a rest.

name: Campfire
description: After a long day's work, it's nice to relax by a campfire and observe the various constellations of the Monster World. Marshmallows are a staple of the Spurrit diet, because they don't present any challenge to the Monster's toothless mouth.

name: Expedition Tent
description: This is the mobile command center of the Spurrits' ongoing studies of the Monster World. All of the Seasonal Monsters' gadgets and instruments are contained therein, and because they have successfully integrated into Monster society, there's no need to camouflage it. As such, the tent is a striking blue color (Spurrit's favorite).

name: Armillary Sphere
description: Finally, a working model of the toroidal Monster World has been created! The moon's orbit passing through the center of the torus is achieved with the help of the various compages that encircle the planet, and the last recorded position of each of the islands of the main dimension has been illustrated in detail. A scientific achievement of epic proportions.

name: Thumpies Totem, Too
description: The magic of the trees that connects the realm of Thingia with the Thumpieverse is also at the root of their shared relationship with the Monster World. That's how we end up with artifacts like this other Thumpies Totem, which tumbled across dimensions to arrive here. While prior incarnations featured other Thumpies, this one celebrates iziT, TawooO, and Anyanka.

name: Hammock
description: Don't be fooled - this Decoration won't add an extra Bed to your Island - that's the job of Castles and Hotels. Still, it's perfect for a lazy snooze.

name: Star Rocks
description: There was some controversy as to what these floating relics should be called. One of the suggestions that was 'floated' around was Rockstars, since they positively radiate Starpower energy. However, Shugabush of Plant Island tells us that means something different where it comes from, so the Monsters went with Star Rocks instead.

name: Bingo Bango Bongos
description: Combine a tropical parasol with some stout drums, and bingo bango bongo, you've got Bingo Bango Bongos.

name: Smunkit
description: A Smunkit, The abbreviated form of a Smunkin-drumkit, is the result of raising Smunkins to enormous size and then capitalizing on their percussive potential.

name: Freed Thing
description: Through the combined efforts and resourcefulness of the singing monsters, one of the Ambered Things has been set loose! Flutter relates that the realm of Thingia, from which it originates, is in fact older than the Monster World, but that it was equally affected by the same events that trapped Kayna, because of the binding magic of the trees. Whatever that means.

name: Striking Bulbs
description: Sure, the name 'Striking Bulbs' could refer to the fact that they're pretty to look at, but those in the know insist it's a reference to their propensity for striking conga drums.

name: Mount Knottshurr
description: Fashioned in the likeness of the mythical Mountain Morsel, Mount Knottshurr is made up of Bubblerite shavings held together with slobber paste. Despite its artificial origin, it seems to have taken on a life of its own. What else could you expect in the magical Monster World?

name: Innertuba
description: The Innertuba's tread-like texture suggests it may have one time been part of a wheel, but we're quite pleased with the new form it's taken.

name: Elmenco Stump
description: The Elmenco Tree grows to a short height, then part of the trunk falls away to reveal a beautiful guitar-shaped carving on the inside.  It inspires dancing of great emotional intensity for all who pluck its strings.

name: Swurlee Tree
description: The Swurlee Tree's great drooping palms resemble candy swirls. The nuts it drops are rumored to be delicious, but they always dissolve before they hit the ground, so no one's ever sampled them.

name: Sweetstreamz Tree
description: Sweetstreamz are made of this: fluffy floating foliage, stupendous streamers of the softest blue, and gleaming stars that dangle and twinkle. Who are we to disagree?

name: PU System
description: Contrary to the traditional PA System, the PU System dispenses odors instead of messages. The inventors claim that there's a huge market for scent-tech... But we're a little apprehensive.

name: Spatial Sapling
description: This is what happens when pollen from outer Space germinates in Natural soil. There's speculation that the giant glowing pod is actually an egg sack, but no one is brave enough to poke it.

name: Boss Monument
description: This superb statue celebrates the five Quad-element Monsters that call the Natural Islands home. Nicknamed 'The Bosses', these dynamic figures serve as role models to the other monsters, and epitomize the cooperative power of harmony.

name: Ockulo Tree
description: This type of tree springs into existence after a Riff has molted and the feathers interlace with local root networks.  Sometimes when it sways in the breeze, it makes a faint guitar shredding sound, prompting Monsters to comment: "Ockulo's Riffed."

name: The Thunker
description: Ahh, nothing like fine art! The Thunker depicts a Monster engaged in Thunking, the act of thinking Thunks, profound thinks that are never spoken aloud. And it even has a pecking bird in the ancient "Art Monstreau" style - divine!

name: Cold Globe
description: Why does everyone insist on calling this decoration a Snow Globe? It’s not like the element is called Snow, it’s called Cold. We’re very insistent on this point.

name: Givutawai Tree
description: Givutawai, givutawai, givutawai now!  This Tree is often the brunt of bogus givaways and suspicious scams... e.g. "You're the one millionth visitor to this Island!  Claim prize now!"  But all it really wants is to share its tiny trinkets, which are a bit more offbeat than their bright packaging might suggest.

name: All's Fair in Love and Song
Grennitch's Gambit
description: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Grennitch's Gambit Quest! Grennitch 'roots' for all Monsters brave enough to represent Plant Island on the Colossingum... but is especially proud of its Champions: Potbelly, Entbrat, Punkleton, and Ghazt.

name: Frigil's Feat
description: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Frigil's Feat Quest! Frigil considers all Monsters brave enough to represent Cold Island on the Colossingum to be pretty 'cool'... but is especially proud of its Champions: Mammott, Deedge, Yool, and Grumpyre.

name: Zeffree's Zenith
description: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Zeffree's Zenith Quest! Zeffree thinks 'highly' of all Monsters brave enough to represent Air Island on the Colossingum... but is especially proud of its Champions: Tweedle, Riff, Schmoochle, and Reebro.

name: Hyddryd's Hurdle
description: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Hyddryd's Hurdle Quest! Hyddryd knows that all Monsters brave enough to represent Water Island on the Colossingum are 'making waves'... but is especially proud of its Champions: Toe Jammer, Shellbeat, Blabbit, and Jeeode.

name: Sollum's Stratagem
description: This is the Trophy that celebrates your victory in the Sollum's Stratagem Quest! Sollum supports all Monsters brave enough to represent Earth Island on the Colossingum... but is especially proud of its Champions: Noggin, Quarrister, Hoola, and Humbug.

name: Log Drum
description: It doesn't take long for a resourceful Monster to grab what's nearby and turn it into an instrument! The Log Drum is a perfect example of this.

name: Spurrit of Adventure Model
description: After dismantling the Spurrit of Adventure sailboat to aid in the construction of the Mess Hall, Mammott, Toe Jammer and Noggin sought to preserve the memory of the faithful little vessel that bore them safely to the Colossingum. This little model does exactly that!

name: Twihorn
description: Who needs a unicorn when you could have a twihorn? Distantly related to pitcher plants, this is more of a pitchy plant. Keep practicing.

name: Costume Rack
description: When the Monsters first arrived on The Colossingum, they discovered a tickle trunk's worth of Costumes to don for their musical competitions. Although they are typically stored inside the Mess Hall, this on-the-go Costume Rack makes quick changes a breeze!

name: Craft Services Table
description: Even though Monsters are focused on training their musical abilities on the Colossingum, it's nice to take a breather and enjoy some refreshments. Watch your fingers, though.

name: Tamborine Trampoline
description: Jumping on a Tamborine Trampoline is a great way to spend the afternoon, but if you're looking for a noiseless activity, this ain't it.

name: Critter Fountain
description: This fountain was created to exalt the adorably fierce critter of the Colossingum, who bounces among the columns and vaulted chambers to stay in 'fighting' shape.

name: Gaung Tree
description: Only the Gaung Tree has branches strong enough to support the weight of this ornate gong. Something to do with the unique metallurgic composition of the disc.

name: Monstrolympic Torch
description: One of the few surviving items other than the Conservatory, this Torch appears to lift Monsters' spirits and put them in a wishing mood whenever they're near. It possibly represents the first time that the magic of the Fire Element was used to create a Torch with powerful potential.

name: Simbull Tree
description: Is that a Clamble you spy? No, but it's thought that this sparsely-leaved but plate-plentiful plant is a different branch of the same family tree.

name: Clarinedge
description: This previously-unnamed plant used to thrive during the era of the Dawn of Fire. The hedge's growth resembles a tangle of clarinets, so in our infinite wisdom, we decided to call it Clarinedge. Aren't we clever?

name: Ottavine
description: The Ottavine is a distant ancestor of the Castanevine, and has a notorious reputation for being finnicky about its growing conditions. We'd call it high-strung, but highfalutin might be more appropriate.

name: Stone Drum
description: It doesn't take long for a persistent Monster to grab an unconventional object and turn it into an instrument! The Stone Drum is a perfect example of this... okay, maybe not a perfect example, but we liked how it went hand in hand with the Log Drum.

name: Strummy-ache
description: This unusual tripodal instrument sounds like a cross between a dulcimer and the rumbling, gurgling sounds a stomach makes when one is hungry. Hence the name Strummy-ache.

name: Conservatory Column Cast - 1/4
description: During the waning years of the Dawn of Fire, two Colossals were troubled, worrying that peace and stability in the Monster World couldn't last forever. While both believed that the best way to prepare for the future was to strengthen the musical abilities of the Singing Monsters, they disagree on how best to go about it.

name: Conservatory Column Cast - 2/4
description: One Colossal, known as Lanysta contended that individual training, conducted in a special environment and focused on honing each Monster's unique musical craft, was the answer. Lanysta found a deserted landmass and imbued it with its lifeforce to create the Colossingum, where Monsters could empower themselves to become better performers in the Conservatory.

name: Conservatory Column Cast - 3/4
description:
This was later revealed to have been a timely decision, as it was not along after t-
(inscription missing)
-age was done, splintering the Continent into many pieces and signaling the end of the Dawn of Fire.

name: Conservatory Column Cast - 4/4
description: The other Colossal, known as Ahymnsa and inspired by the actions of their friend, chose another landmass to shift their power into, taking along a piece of the amber containing an important figure to act as the island's centerpiece. There, Ahymnsa created a prophecy that foretold of a time when Monsters singing together and being united in song would help to restore balance in the Monster World.

name: Waveform - Splashy Sunken Treasure
description: This special Decoration stands as a testament to our special Clubbox clout when you help Hype up the performance of Waveform! Outside of its duties as Autotuna, the T-Pirainha is an avid collector of artifacts and sunken treasure, some dating all the way back to the era of the Dawn of Fire!

name: Waveform - Flashy Geometric Visualizers
description: This special Decoration stands as a testament to our special Clubbox clout when you help Hype up the performance of Waveform! Initially created as visualizers for the sonic stability of Clubbox concerts, these floating geometric forms are cool enough to serve as entertainment in their own right!

name: Waveform - Steamy Holo Projections
description: This special Decoration stands as a testament to our special Clubbox clout when you help Hype up the performance of Waveform! If there's one thing that can be said about T-Pirainha and its cronies, it's that that their fasion sense is beyond compare — and these holograms are (not-quite solid) proof!

name: Pomily Family Band - M-Pop Centerpiece
description: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of the Pomily Family Band! Each member of the Pomily is a star in its own right, but it's so much fun to see them all perform together!

name: Pomily Family Band - Flower Centerpiece
description: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of the Pomily Family Band! Flowers are the perfect expression of nature's beauty; for the Pomily, they also represent the power of peace and working together!

name: Pomily Family Band - Bubblegum Centerpiece
description: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of the Pomily Family Band! What better way to recognize the unique relationship between the Pomily and the different foods they eat than a multicolored monument to munchies?

name: BlitherPhish - Punk Skatepark
description: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of BlitherPhish! Even if your Act is all about being tough and edgy, don't forget to be kind and appreciative of your adoring fans!

name: BlitherPhish - Mecha Manipulator
description: This special Decoration stands as a testament to your Clubbox clout when you helped Hype up the performance of BlitherPhish! These impressive contraptions rival the technological might of the Clubbox itself - could they be related somehow?
